channel.title: Using pre-existing WordPress themes w/ Frontity?
channel.description:
Hello all! I'm in the process of deciding whether or not I wish to use Frontity with an important upcoming project of time, and I haven't been able to find solid documentation on how Frontity works with existing WordPress themes.
I know that the popular ones and the custom Frontity ones are an option, but what about themes you get from third parties? Specifically in my case, this theme: https://demos.upperthemes.com/imperio/
I have a hunch my organization will want to use this theme if possible, so please get back to me if you have any advice and have a good day!

item.title: Using pre-existing WordPress themes w/ Frontity?
item.dc:creator: thewizardofwicacy
item.description:
I think what he is asking is, is there a way to duplicate the look of the theme? Such as, by using the html from the theme to render components.

Read full topic
item.source: Using pre-existing WordPress themes w/ Frontity?

item.title: Using pre-existing WordPress themes w/ Frontity?
item.dc:creator: Pablo
item.description:
Hey @nicholas Frontity is a framework to build React Frontends for WordPress. This means that traditional PHP themes are not compatible.

Read full topic
item.source: Using pre-existing WordPress themes w/ Frontity?

item.title: Using pre-existing WordPress themes w/ Frontity?
item.dc:creator: nicholas
item.description:
Hello all! I’m in the process of deciding whether or not I wish to use Frontity with an important upcoming project of time, and I haven’t been able to find solid documentation on how Frontity works with existing WordPress themes.

I know that the popular ones and the custom Frontity ones are an option, but what about themes you get from third parties? Specifically in my case, this theme: https://demos.upperthemes.com/imperio/

I have a hunch my organization will want to use this theme if possible, so please get back to me if you have any advice and have a good day!

Read full topic
item.source: Using pre-existing WordPress themes w/ Frontity?
